what_it_does: Implements a least-privilege Supabase authenticated principal: it logs in via email/password or refresh_token, caches the returned JWT, refreshes it within 30 seconds of expiry, and exposes PostgREST/Realtime auth headers (apikey anon key + Authorization Bearer). The class is parameterized by an environment prefix so the same primitive can serve multiple internal service accounts.
observations: Uses only the Python standard library (urllib, json, time, base64, os).
Env resolution is prefix-first with shared SUPABASE_URL / SUPABASE_ANON_KEY fallbacks.
Supports password grant (preferred) and refresh_token grant, raising SupabaseAuthError if neither is configured.
Caches the access token and refreshes when expiry is within 30 seconds.
Provides an injected `fetch` seam so the auth flow can be unit-tested offline.
Includes an `if __name__ == '__main__'` self-test using a hand-made JWT and fake fetch.
Extracts the principal uid from the JWT `sub` claim by base64url decoding without signature verification.
Fail-loud design: missing credentials, failed auth POSTs, and malformed responses all raise SupabaseAuthError.
standouts: Single reusable class parameterized by env prefix to avoid per-component copies of the same login flow.
Injected `fetch` seam makes the Supabase token endpoint unit-testable without network access.
Explicit fail-loud contract: no silent unauthenticated calls.
conventions: Follows least-privilege/RLS-scoped service-account pattern instead of the service-role master key.
Follows env-based configuration with prefix namespacing.
Uses broad `except Exception` in `jwt_sub` with a `noqa` suppression.
Uses `noqa: S310` to suppress bandit warning on `urlopen`.
Breaks from using the official supabase-py client by calling the raw /auth/v1/token endpoint.
concerns: `jwt_sub` catches a broad Exception and re-raises as SupabaseAuthError, hiding the original failure shape.
Token expiry is computed from `expires_in` rather than the token's own `exp` claim, so clock skew or a shortened token could lead to using an expired JWT.
`_post_token` treats any status below 300 as success without checking for unexpected 2xx codes.
The 30-second refresh margin and 10-second request timeout are hard-coded and not configurable.
The self-test asserts against a real-looking Supabase project URL placeholder.
`_get` treats empty strings as missing values, which may hide accidentally blank env vars.
notes: A minimal, dependency-free, env-prefix-parameterized Supabase login primitive built to be fail-loud and offline-testable.
questions: Is the Supabase URL used in the self-test a real project or an intentionally fake placeholder?
How are SA_EMAIL/SA_PASSWORD provisioned securely to each component at deployment time?
Which components besides COMPANY_CHANNEL and VI_VISION are expected to consume this class?
Is JWT signature verification intentionally omitted because callers trust their own freshly-issued tokens, or should verification happen elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a reusable, least-privilege Supabase principal that authenticates, caches, refreshes, and supplies scoped Bearer headers, with env-prefix parameterization and fail-loud behavior.
intention_signals: It appears designed to be the single, shared home for Supabase authenticated-principal logic so every internal component can obtain its own scoped JWT without duplicating the login/refresh/cache code, while remaining unit-testable and never silently falling back to unauthenticated requests.
novelty: A tiny, dependency-free Supabase principal class with an injected fetch seam and prefix-parameterized env resolution, explicitly positioned as the one reusable home for this auth pattern.
contribution: Provides a reusable, testable, fail-loud Supabase authenticated-principal abstraction that a fused system can keep to consolidate JWT login, caching, and refresh behavior across components while keeping credentials scoped and the anon key separate.
summary_for_embedding: A dependency-free Python class that authenticates a dedicated Supabase principal via password or refresh token, caches and refreshes the JWT, and produces PostgREST/Realtime Bearer headers, parameterized by an environment prefix.
intention_for_embedding: To create a single, reusable, least-privilege Supabase authentication primitive for multiple internal components without duplicating the JWT login flow.

symbols.does: Custom exception raised when principal authentication fails.
symbols.description: A RuntimeError subclass used throughout the module to enforce fail-loud behavior; any missing credential, failed auth request, or malformed token response is surfaced as this exception.

symbols.does: Decodes a base64url-encoded JSON segment into a dict.
symbols.description: A small helper that adds padding and decodes a JWT payload segment; used by jwt_sub to read the `sub` claim without verifying the token signature.

symbols.does: Returns the `sub` claim from an access token by decoding its payload.
symbols.description: Extracts the principal uid from the JWT body using base64url decoding; it does not verify the signature and raises SupabaseAuthError if the token is malformed.
The main abstraction: reads prefix-scoped env vars, chooses a grant type, fetches and caches a JWT, refreshes it near expiry, and produces PostgREST/Realtime auth headers.

symbols.does: Initializes the principal with an env prefix, optional env dict, and optional fetch seam.
symbols.description: Stores the prefix, env source, injected fetch function, and an internal token cache; the fetch seam enables offline unit testing of the full auth flow.

symbols.does: Reads a prefix-scoped env var with shared SUPABASE_* fallbacks.
symbols.description: Looks up `{prefix}_{key}` first, then any supplied fallback keys, returning the first truthy value found; treats empty strings as missing.

symbols.does: Returns the configured Supabase project URL.
symbols.description: Resolves the project URL from `{prefix}_SUPABASE_URL` or the shared `SUPABASE_URL` env var.

symbols.does: Returns the configured Supabase anon/public API key.
symbols.description: Resolves the anon key from `{prefix}_ANON_KEY` or the shared `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` env var; required for the apikey PostgREST header.

symbols.does: Selects the authentication grant type and payload from env credentials.
symbols.description: Prefers email+password (`grant_type=password`) and falls back to a refresh token (`grant_type=refresh_token`); raises SupabaseAuthError if neither is configured.

symbols.does: POSTs to the Supabase /auth/v1/token endpoint to obtain tokens.
symbols.description: Builds the token request with the anon key in the `apikey` header, sends JSON credentials, and parses the response; raises SupabaseAuthError on network failure, HTTP error, or non-JSON response.

symbols.does: Returns a valid, cached or freshly refreshed access token.
symbols.description: Returns the cached JWT if it is not near expiry; otherwise calls the configured grant flow, validates that an `access_token` is present, stores it with an expiry timestamp, and returns it.

symbols.does: Returns PostgREST/Realtime headers using the anon key and Bearer JWT.
symbols.description: Produces a dict with `apikey` set to the anon key and `Authorization` set to `Bearer <access_token>`; raises SupabaseAuthError if the anon key is missing.

symbols.does: Returns the principal uid from the current access token.
symbols.description: Convenience wrapper around jwt_sub that uses the cached or freshly fetched access token to return the JWT `sub` claim for RLS/audit use.

symbols.does: Injected test fetch function used in the offline self-test.
symbols.description: A closure in `__main__` that increments a call counter, asserts expected grant/url/anon values, and returns a hand-made JWT response so the module can self-test without network access.
